paper_title: A Compositional Study (pXRF) of Early Holocene Obsidian Assemblages from Cyprus, Eastern Mediterranean

methods.description: This method involves the use of portable X-ray Fluorescence Spectrometry (pXRF) to analyze obsidian artefacts for elemental characterization. The method is chosen for its precision, reliability, and non-destructive nature, making it ideal for mass sampling within museum premises where sampling is prohibited.

steps.description: The analyses were undertaken using a handheld Olympus Innov-X Delta XRF device (HH pXRF).
steps.searchable_summary: use handheld Olympus Innov-X Delta XRF device for analysis

steps.description: The model operates at 40 kV and is equipped with the Delta Tantalum (Ta) anode X-Ray tube, and uses a Silicon Drift Detector.
steps.searchable_summary: configure XRF device with 40 kV, Ta anode, and Silicon Drift Detector

steps.description: The 'Soil' mode, which captures elements ranging from P and higher (the preferred range for obsidian) were chosen for the analyses.
Each artefact was analysed for 80s of total exposure in 'Soil' mode.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze each artefact for 80 seconds in Soil mode

steps.description: The artefacts were positioned to completely cover the pXRF detector window, usually with their most flat surface.
steps.searchable_summary: position artefacts to cover pXRF detector window

steps.description: In the rare cases where the artefact was smaller than the pXRF detector window, it was placed in a way as to cover/overlay the maximum diameter of the X-ray beam.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust artefact position for smaller artefacts

steps.description: The artefacts were placed on the manufacturer provided test stand and controlled through a computer and camera.
steps.searchable_summary: use test stand and control artefact positioning with computer and camera

steps.description: The accuracy of the applied mode was ensured through the USGS RGM-2 and NIST612 certified standard reference materials (SRMs).
steps.searchable_summary: validate accuracy using USGS RGM-2 and NIST612 SRMs

steps.description: All data was processed in Excel.
The quantification is based on a Fundamental Parameters algorithm designed by the manufacturer (Innov-X), with results given as ppm.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify data using Fundamental Parameters algorithm
